perturbation.original_text: Sponsor shall pay $*** to SDBF pursuant to the following: $ *** due on or before April 30, 2008 $ *** due on or before June 30, 2008 $ *** due on or before August 31, 2008
perturbation.changed_text: Sponsor shall pay $*** to SDBF pursuant to the following: $ *** due on or before April 30, 2008 $ *** due on or before July 31, 2008 $ *** due on or before August 31, 2008
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text specifies a payment due on or before June 30, 2008, while the modified text changes the date to July 31, 2008. This contradicts the payment schedule established, creating uncertainty regarding the due dates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. In consideration of the Sponsorship Benefits to be furnished by SDBF to Sponsor hereunder, Sponsor shall pay to SDBF the Annual Payments set forth in the Agreement Summary on the dates set forth therein. Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within fifteen (15) days after such payment is due shall bear interest from the date the payment was due until paid, at a per annum rate of interest equal to the prime rate of interest as published in the Wall Street Journal, plus five (5) percentage points, or if such interest rate is not permitted by applicable law, at the highest non-usurious interest rate permitted by applicable law. At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due and may exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a payment due date of June 30, 2008. The changed text alters this to July 31, 2008. The contradicted text states that the Sponsor shall pay the Annual Payments on the dates set forth in the Agreement Summary. By changing a date, the modified text directly contradicts the original payment schedule and the requirement in the contradicted text to adhere to that schedule, creating an inconsistency in payment obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within fifteen (15) days after such payment is due shall bear interest from the date the payment was due until paid, at a per annum rate of interest equal to the prime rate of interest as published in the Wall Street Journal, plus five (5) percentage points, or if such interest rate is not permitted by applicable law, at the highest non-usurious interest rate permitted by applicable law. At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due and may exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.changed_text: Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within thirty (30) days after such payment is due shall bear interest from the date the payment was due until paid, at a per annum rate of interest equal to the prime rate of interest as published in the Wall Street Journal, plus five (5) percentage points, or if such interest rate is not permitted by applicable law, at the highest non-usurious interest rate permitted by applicable law. At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due and may exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.explanation: The 'Payments' section originally stated that interest would be applied if payment wasn't received within fifteen (15) days, but has been changed to thirty (30) days. This contradicts with the section indicating that SDBF may terminate the agreement if payment is not made within fifteen (15) days, creating uncertainty in enforcement. This creates an in-text contradiction within section 3a, between the grace period for late payment interest accrual and termination rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a. In consideration of the Sponsorship Benefits to be furnished by SDBF to Sponsor hereunder, Sponsor shall pay to SDBF the Annual Payments set forth in the Agreement Summary on the dates set forth therein. Any payment due pursuant to this Section that is not received by SDBF within thirty (30) days after such payment is due shall bear interest from the date the payment was due until paid, at a per annum rate of interest equal to the prime rate of interest as published in the Wall Street Journal, plus five (5) percentage points, or if such interest rate is not permitted by applicable law, at the highest non-usurious interest rate permitted by applicable law. At its sole option, SDBF may terminate this Agreement in the event that a payment required hereunder is not made within fifteen (15) days of the date such payment is due and may exercise all other rights and remedies available to SDBF.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the grace period for payment from fifteen (15) days to thirty (30) days for interest accrual, while the termination clause remains at fifteen (15) days. This creates a direct contradiction within the 'Payments' section (section 3a, as per the explanation) because SDBF can terminate the agreement if payment is not made within fifteen (15) days, but interest will not accrue until thirty (30) days have passed, creating an inconsistency in enforcement and timelines. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies' definition, as different sections of the contract provide conflicting deadlines or obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of cancellation, non-renewal or material modification SDBF shall receive thirty (30) days written notice thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of cancellation, non-renewal or material modification SDBF shall receive ten (10) days written notice thereof.
perturbation.explanation: This creates an in-text contradiction. This section requires Sponsor to maintain insurance with SDBF as an additional insured. If the insurance policy is canceled, Sponsor should notify SDBF in thirty (30) days written notice, now reduced to ten (10) days written notice. This contradicts with the original intent of giving SDBF enough time to find insurance if cancellation happens.This will cause issues, regarding when SDBF gets a notification, leading to a contradiction with the original intent.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sponsor must obtain, and continuously maintain, at its own expense, the following insurance policies: 1. Workers' Compensation in compliance with California's laws, including Employers' Liability with minimum limits of: $ *** Each Accident; $ *** Disease - Each Employee; $ *** Disease - Policy Limit. 2. An Insurance Services Office occurrence based Commercial General Liability Insurance Policy, including contractual liability and products/completed operations liability coverage with minimum limits of: $ *** Each Occurrence; $ *** General Aggregate; $ *** Products/Completed Operations Aggregate. All insurance policies must be issued by an admitted insurance carrier with an A.M. Best rating of A-8 or better. SDBF, Padres LP, the City of San Diego and each of their subsidiary or affiliated companies and its and their directors, officers and employees must be named as Additional Insureds under the Commercial General Liability, Automobile Liability and Umbrella Liability Policies. All of these policies must contain Cross Liability Endorsements, or their equivalent. Further, coverage for the Additional Insureds shall apply on a primary basis irrespective of any other insurance, whether collectible or not. All policies shall be endorsed to provide a Waiver of Subrogation in favor of SDBF. In the event of cancellation, non-renewal or material modification SDBF shall receive ten (10) days written notice thereof. Sponsor shall provide SDBF with certificates of insurance evidencing compliance with all insurance provisions noted above prior to the commencement of the sponsorship and annually prior to the expiration of each required insurance policy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notification period for cancellation, non-renewal, or material modification of the insurance policy from thirty (30) days to ten (10) days. The contradicted text is in the insurance section and requires SDBF to receive notice of cancellation. The reduction in the notification period directly contradicts the original intent of providing SDBF with sufficient time to secure alternative insurance coverage, creating a clear inconsistency in the agreement as outlined in the explanation.
